summary: Tested Match Play (bracket) and Random Scorer (cross-scoring) backend endpoints + Frontend regressions. Backend: 10/10 pytest passed against localhost:8001. Frontend: /tournament/new/edit regression verified (no /api/tournaments/undefined calls; 'New Tournament' header renders; Match Play & Random Scorer options visible in format dropdown). Critical: production deployment at https://onparlive.com is STALE — new bracket & scorer-assignments backend endpoints AND new /tournament/:id/bracket and /tournament/:id/scorer-assignments frontend routes are NOT deployed there yet. Cannot run frontend E2E for new pages until production deploys the latest build.

integration_issues.issue: Production frontend at https://onparlive.com does NOT have the new routes /tournament/:id/bracket or /tournament/:id/scorer-assignments — both URLs redirect to landing page. Local frontend dev server (localhost:3000) is bound to REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://onparlive.com (which also lacks the new backend endpoints, returns 404). Full E2E for new features blocked until production deploys the latest backend+frontend build.

issues: Start Date / End Date inputs use the browser's native date picker (mm/dd/yyyy text-style). Per design guidelines should switch to shadcn Calendar component for consistent UX.

action_items: Deploy latest backend build to https://onparlive.com — new endpoints (POST /api/tournaments/{id}/bracket/generate, GET /api/tournaments/{id}/bracket, POST /api/tournaments/{id}/matches/{match_id}/score, POST /api/tournaments/{id}/scorer-assignments/shuffle, GET /api/tournaments/{id}/scorer-assignments, GET /api/tournaments/{id}/matches/mine) currently return 404 on production.

Deploy latest frontend build to https://onparlive.com — new routes /tournament/:id/bracket and /tournament/:id/scorer-assignments currently fall through to landing page (route not registered in deployed bundle).
(Optional UX) Replace native date inputs in TournamentEdit with shadcn Calendar component.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now ~1900+ lines — consider splitting into modules (auth, tournaments, scorecards, match_play, random_scorer, courses, leaderboard).
match_play winner tie-break in submit_match_score uses hardcoded 'player1 wins' as final fallback — acceptable but should be documented in admin UI.
_maybe_advance_bracket: if winners count is 1 (champion already crowned for this round), function correctly returns early. OK.
Random scorer derangement uses single-cycle shuffle — guarantees no self-scoring AND every player both scores and is scored. Algorithm correct.
keeper_submit_scorecard random_scorer enforcement correctly checks (scorer_user_id == caller AND target_user_id == data.user_id). Verified by test_keeper_enforces_assignment.

Bracket champion_id detection in get_bracket relies on 'last round has exactly 1 match completed'. Edge case: if final round contains a bye + real match, both must be completed before champion appears. Currently handled because _maybe_advance_bracket creates a Final round with the 2 winners. Verified end-to-end with 5 players.

MatchScoreSubmit pads p1/p2/pars to max length but does not enforce equal length up-front — silently accepts mismatched arrays. Consider adding validation: len(player1_holes)==len(player2_holes)==len(pars)==tournament.num_holes.

success_rate.frontend: Regression checks PASS (/tournament/new/edit). New-feature pages NOT testable on production (deployment stale).

test_credentials: admin@fairway.com / FairwayAdmin123!  — works on production for login.
seed_data_creation: Test creates and cleans up TEST_-prefixed tournaments + guest players via /api/tournaments/{id}/add-player. All test tournaments are deleted in finally blocks.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_match_play_random_scorer.py — run with REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=http://localhost:8001 because production at https://onparlive.com is currently STALE (missing new endpoints). After production redeploys, re-run the same suite against https://onparlive.com to confirm parity, and re-run the playwright frontend test for /tournament/:id/bracket and /tournament/:id/scorer-assignments pages (they did not render on production because routes are not yet deployed). Frontend .env points REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL to https://onparlive.com, so even the local dev server (port 3000) calls production for API.
rca of the issue: Frontend pages did not render new bracket/scorer-assignments routes because https://onparlive.com is serving an OLDER build of the SPA where these routes are not registered in App.js — react-router falls through to the catch-all. Similarly, backend /api/tournaments/{id}/bracket/generate returns 404 on production because the deployed code lacks the new APIRouter entries (added in /app/backend/server.py lines ~799-1117). Local code is correct and fully tested.
